defends: The IOPHON ~/.claude §2 layout: each kind of file has exactly one canonical subdirectory. A file of a known kind sitting anywhere else is drift.

rules.desc: skill / slash-command files (only .md carrying skill frontmatter)

note: FIXED scope (WORKING_METHOD §1). Rules are ordered specific -> generic: the first rule whose globs match the basename wins, so audit_*.md is classified as an audit, not swept by the generic *.md skill rule. The skill rule is the only one that also requires skill frontmatter, so an ordinary .md note is left alone rather than moved to commands/. Files matching NO rule are not drift — cleanup only moves kinds it has a canonical home for.
